2604.24222.title: MEMCoder: Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory for Private-Library-Oriented Code Generation
2604.24222.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at general code generation, but their performance drops sharply in enterprise settings that rely on internal private libraries absent from public pre-training corpora. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) offers a training-free alternative by providing static API documentation, we find that such documentation typically provides only isolated definitions, leaving a fundamental knowledge gap. Specifically, LLMs struggle with a task-level lack of coordination patterns between APIs and an API-level misunderstanding of parameter constraints and boundary conditions. To address this, we propose MEMCoder, a novel framework that enables LLMs to autonomously accumulate and evolve Usage Guidelines across these two dimensions. MEMCoder introduces a Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory that captures distilled lessons from the model's own problem-solving trajectories. During inference, MEMCoder employs a dual-source retrieval mechanism to inject both static documentation and relevant historical guidelines into the context. The framework operates in an automated closed loop by using objective execution feedback to reflect on successes and failures, resolve knowledge conflicts, and dynamically update memory. Extensive evaluations on the NdonnxEval and NumbaEval benchmarks demonstrate that MEMCoder substantially enhances existing RAG systems, yielding an average absolute pass@1 gain of 16.31%. Furthermore, MEMCoder exhibits vastly superior domain-specific adaptation compared to existing memory-based continual learning methods.
2604.24222.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在一般代碼生成方面表現優異，但在依賴於內部私有庫的企業環境中，其性能急劇下降，這些私有庫在公共預訓練語料中並不存在。雖然檢索增強生成（RAG）通過提供靜態API文檔提供了一種無需訓練的替代方案，但我們發現這種文檔通常僅提供孤立的定義，留下了基本的知識空白。具體而言，LLMs在API之間缺乏協調模式的任務級別上存在困難，並且在API級別上對參數約束和邊界條件存在誤解。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了MEMCoder，一個新穎的框架，使LLMs能夠自主積累和演化這兩個維度的使用指南。MEMCoder引入了一種多維度演變記憶，捕捉模型自身問題解決軌跡中的提煉教訓。在推理過程中，MEMCoder採用雙來源檢索機制，將靜態文檔和相關歷史指南注入上下文中。該框架通過使用客觀執行反饋在自動閉環中運作，反思成功與失敗，解決知識衝突，並動態更新記憶。在NdonnxEval和NumbaEval基準上的廣泛評估表明，MEMCoder顯著增強了現有的RAG系統，平均絕對pass@1增益達到16.31%。此外，與現有的基於記憶的持續學習方法相比，MEMCoder顯示出極其優越的領域特定適應性。
2604.24222.author: Mofei Li et.al.
2604.24222.authors: Mofei Li, Taozhi Chen, Guowei Yang, Jia Li
